headline: GOLD SURGES to $4,850, Silver ROCKETS 7% to $77 — US-Iran Ceasefire Sparks Precious Metals Reversal
snippet: Gold hit $4,850/oz (+3%) and silver surged to $77/oz (+7%) on April 8 after the US-Iran ceasefire sent oil below $100/bbl, weakened the dollar, and revived rate-cut expectations. Silver's outperformance marks its highest level since March 18.

headline: Zijin Seals C$5.5B Allied Gold Buyout — Chinese Giant Adds 400,000 oz/yr Across Three African Mines
snippet: Zijin Gold International agreed to acquire Canada's Allied Gold in an all-cash deal at C$44/share (27% premium), adding open-pit mines in Mali, Ivory Coast, and Ethiopia. The Kurmuk project in Ethiopia targets 290,000 oz/yr at a $950/oz AISC from mid-2026.

headline: Americas Gold & Silver HITS 4,896 g/t Ag at Galena — Launches Record 64,000m 2026 Drill Program
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (TSX: USA) discovered 10 new high-grade veins at the Idaho Galena Complex — including 4,896 g/t Ag and 3.95% Cu over 1.3m — and announced the company's largest-ever drilling campaign: 64,000m across Galena and Cosalá in 2026.

headline: Dryden Gold Drills 32.87 g/t Au Over 4.25m — THREE New High-Grade Zones Found at Big Master, Ontario
snippet: Dryden Gold (TSX-V: DRY) drilled 4.25m at 32.87 g/t Au (including 0.50m at 252 g/t) and a second hole of 4.00m at 6.51 g/t Au at the Big Master system — step-outs 300m from known zones, confirming the system extends well beyond current boundaries.

headline: China VACUUMING Silver — 790 Tonnes Imported in First Two Months of 2026, an Eight-Year Record
snippet: China's silver imports hit an 8-year record of 790 tonnes in Jan-Feb 2026, driven by solar manufacturers front-loading production before the April 1 export rebate cut and a surge in retail investor buying. Beijing also moved to require approval for silver exports.

headline: Ghana DOUBLES Gold Royalties to 12% Sliding Scale — Miners Warn of Chilling Effect on New Investment
snippet: Ghana replaced its flat 5% royalty with a 5%–12% sliding scale tied to gold prices — triggering the top rate immediately, as gold trades above $4,500. Major miners and diplomats from the US and China lobbied against the measure; Ghana's government rejected all amendments.

headline: Core Lithium Greenlights Finniss Restart — $205M Fully Funded, First Ore in May, Concentrate by Q4
snippet: Core Lithium (ASX: CXO) approved the final investment decision for its Finniss project after securing $205M in funding. Open-pit mining at the Grants deposit begins May 2026, with first spodumene concentrate targeted for Q4 2026. Full 1.2Mtpa ramp-up by mid-2028.

headline: F3 Uranium Hits 2.50% U₃O₈ Over 1.0m at Athabasca Broach — Tetra Zone Extends to 135m Plunge
snippet: F3 Uranium (TSX-V: FUU) reported hole PLN25-205 returning 1.0m of 2.50% U₃O₈ within a 22.5m interval at the Broach Tetra Zone, 12km NW of Paladin's Triple R. The mineralized plunge extended from 60m to 135m, with ~300m of untested shear remaining up-dip.

headline: Uranium Spot at $84.55/lb — AI Datacenter Power Demand Creates 'Wall of Demand' Through 2045
snippet: U3O8 spot closed at $84.55/lb the week of April 7 after peaking at $101.41 in January. Industry analysts warn that uncovered utility requirements — demand not yet locked in by long-term contracts — have reached record levels, with AI-driven electricity demand accelerating the supply deficit timeline.

headline: COPPER Climbs to $5.87/lb on Ceasefire Optimism — But LME Stockpiles Hit 8-Year High in Warning Sign
snippet: Copper rose to $5.87/lb (+2.1%) on April 10, heading for a third straight weekly gain on Iran ceasefire optimism. The rally faces a headwind: LME warehouse stockpiles climbed to an 8-year high, signalling subdued physical demand at current price levels.

headline: MINERS MINTING MONEY: Newmont Posts Record $7.3B Free Cash Flow — 70% Margins as Gold Rivals Tech Sector
snippet: With gold above $5,000 and average AISC around $1,800/oz, senior producers are generating $3,000+ margins per ounce — unprecedented in mining history. Newmont posted $7.3B in free cash flow, with gross profit margins approaching 70%, drawing comparisons to big-tech profitability.

headline: Junior Miners Take Centre Stage as Record Gold Triggers Hunt for High-Grade Discovery
snippet: Gold's near-$5,600 peak in Q1 2026 has revived institutional appetite for early-stage exploration plays. Analysts note that mining equities still trade at a discount to NAV despite record metal prices, with GDX up just 35-40% in a period where spot gold gained 60%+ — a gap that rarely persists.

headline: Mining M&A Hits 13-Year High — $94B in 2025 Deals Sets Up Bigger Wave of Consolidation in 2026
snippet: Global mining M&A totalled $93.7B in 2025 — the highest yearly total since 2012 — with gold the dominant target and copper deals rising sharply. January 2026 alone saw $7.1B in transactions, and analysts at Bain predict the next wave will be larger and more decisive.
